summary: Parses a delimited assignment string into an object of decoded key-value entries.
signature_intent: Accepts a string plus optional parsing settings and returns an object mapping parsed keys to decoded values.
behavior: Requires the first argument to be a string.
Splits the input into candidate assignments using an internal pair separator.
Ignores segments that do not contain an equals sign.
Uses the text before the first equals sign as the key and the remaining text as the value.
Trims surrounding whitespace from parsed keys and values.
Removes wrapping double quotes from quoted values.
Decodes values with a provided decoder when available, otherwise with the default decoder.
Keeps the first value for a key and ignores later duplicates.
Returns an object containing the parsed entries.
edge_cases: Throws a TypeError when the first argument is not a string.
An absent settings argument uses default behavior.
Empty or separator-only input returns an empty object unless valid assignments are present.
Assignments with empty keys or empty values are still parsed.
Only the first equals sign separates the key from the value.
Duplicate keys preserve the earliest decoded value.
forbidden_details: Original parameter or local variable names.
Repository names or source file paths.
Internal helper names or regular expression identifiers.
Unnecessary implementation quirks not needed to describe behavior.
